name: H2
description: H2 is an embeddable Java SQL database engine. It runs in-process as a library with zero external dependencies, supports both disk-based and in-memory databases, and provides a JDBC API for Java applications. It features a browser-based console, encryption, full-text search, multi-version concurrency control, and a tiny footprint of around 2.5 MB. H2 is widely used in the Java ecosystem as an embedded database for development, testing, and lightweight production deployments.
core_strengths: Embedded in-process operation with zero external dependencies
Ultra-fast in-memory mode for testing and transient workloads
Small footprint: around 2.5 MB jar file size
Full JDBC API with broad SQL standard support
Multi-version concurrency control and transaction support
best_use_cases: Embedded database inside Java desktop applications
In-memory database for fast integration testing in CI/CD pipelines
Development-time stand-in for heavier RDBMS like PostgreSQL or MySQL
Local storage for Spring Boot microservices running in dev mode
Lightweight SQL engine for Java-based IoT and edge deployments
remarks: H2 can also run in server mode (TCP) for multi-connection access, though its primary strength is embedded operation. The dual MPL 2.0 / EPL 1.0 license requires modified H2 source code to be published; using unmodified H2 in commercial applications is allowed. The jdbc:h2:mem: URL creates a private in-memory database per connection; for a named in-memory database shared across connections, use jdbc:h2:mem:<name>.
